headline: 'In 1979, the Iranian left played a major role in the rise of the Islamic Republic'
subhead: Philosopher Stéphanie Roza and political scientist Amirpasha Tavakkoli explain how, throughout the 20th century, the political turmoil in Iran, torn between modernity and anti-modernity, ultimately led to Islamist victory.

headline: Trump says US bombed Iran's Kharg Island, threatens oil infrastructure
subhead: Trump announced that the US 'totally obliterated every military target' in Iran's 'crown jewel' Kharg Island, which handles almost all of Iran's crude exports. In response to the latest attack, Iran's armed forces threatened on Saturday to turn US-linked oil infrastructure 'into a pile of ashes' if Iran's energy facilities were attacked.

headline: The Gulf States tighten grip on wartime communication
subhead: Arrests and pressure are mounting in the oil monarchies, which are facing Iranian airstrikes for the first time. Tourists, as well as journalists, are being accused of 'spreading panic' by sharing footage of the damage.

headline: Eric Fassin, sociologist: 'The Epstein case reveals the absolute power of men who, by flaunting their transgressions, declared themselves above the law'
subhead: The sociologist describes the Epstein case as 'a circle of male power that is closing ranks against feminist politics.' By exercising this violent domination, money, sexuality, knowledge and relationships become interchangeable forms of capital.

headline: Advocates push to end ban on athletes with intellectual disabilities for 2030 Winter Paralympics
subhead: While no athletes with intellectual disabilities compete in the Milan-Cortina 2026 Winter Games, a campaign is underway to have adapted sports reinstated in the Paralympic program for 2030, when the Games will be held in the French Alps.

subhead: Considered by the authorities in his country to be one of the most important cocaine traffickers in Europe, Flor Bressers, a Belgian with an atypical profile, fond of luxury and architect of sprawling networks, is at the center of a legal saga.

headline: Benjamin Netanyahu, a prime minister without checks and balances
subhead: The strikes in Iran have further reduced the political space for opposition in Israel, even as concerns mount over a possible ground offensive in Lebanon. Today, only Donald Trump seems capable of influencing the Israeli leader.

headline: Jeffrey Epstein's Parisian lives
subhead: Over 18 years, the extremely kitsch property at 22 Avenue Foch served Epstein as a European office, a salon for artists and scientists and, above all, one of the places where he committed his sexual crimes.
